sourceMovie.title: R.I.P.D.
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto R.I.P.D. (2013) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Nice Guys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If R.I.P.D. clicked for you, The Nice Guys (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If R.I.P.D. clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after R.I.P.D.?
faq.answer: Try The Nice Guys, Hot Fuzz, Who Framed Roger Rabbit—each shares DNA with R.I.P.D. while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is R.I.P.D.?
faq.answer: R.I.P.D. (2013) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is R.I.P.D. part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "R.I.P.D. Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
